items.title: Sectoral Deployment of Bank Credit – March 2026
items.summary: Data on sectoral deployment of bank credit for the month of March 2026, collected from 41 select scheduled commercial banks (SCBs) which together account for about 95 per cent of the total non-food credit by all SCBs 1 , are set out in Statements I and II . On a year-on-year (y-o-y) basis, non-food bank credit 2 grew by 15.9 per cent as on the fortnight ended March 31, 2026, compared to 10.9 per cent during the corresponding fortnight of the previous year (i.e., April 04, 2025). Highlights of the sectoral deployment of bank credit as on the fortnight ended March 31, 2026 are given below: Credi

items.title: Lending and Deposit Rates of Scheduled Commercial Banks – April 2026
items.summary: Data on lending and deposit rates of scheduled commercial banks (SCBs) (excluding regional rural banks and small finance banks) received during the month of April 2026 are set out in Tables 1 to 9. Highlights: Lending Rates: The weighted average lending rate (WALR) on fresh rupee loans of SCBs stood at 8.40 per cent in March 2026 (8.44 per cent in February 2026). The WALR on outstanding rupee loans of SCBs marginally declined to 8.99 per cent in March 2026 from 9.00 per cent in February 2026. 1-Year median Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate (MCLR) of SCBs increased to 8.55 per cent in A

items.title: RBI approves the voluntary amalgamation of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) with TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra)
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) with TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra). The Scheme has been sanctioned in exercise of the powers conferred under sub-section (4) of Section 44A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The Scheme will come into force with effect from May 04, 2026 (Monday). The branches of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) will function as branches of TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra) with effect from May 04, 2026. (Brij Raj) Chief General Manage

items.title: Final redemption under Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme - Redemption Price for final redemption of SGB 2018-19 Series-I due on May 04, 2026
items.summary: In terms of GOI notification F.No.4(8)-W&M/2018 dated April 13, 2018 (SGB 2018-19 Series-I-Issue date May 04, 2018) on Sovereign Gold Bond Scheme, the Gold Bond shall be repayable on the expiration of eight years from the date of issue of the Gold Bonds. Accordingly, the final redemption date of the above tranche shall be May 04, 2026. 2. Further, the redemption price of SGB shall be based on simple average of closing price of gold of 999 purity of previous three business days from the date of redemption, as published by the India Bullion and Jewellers Association Ltd (IBJA). Accordingly, the

items.title: 46th Half-Yearly Report on Management of Foreign Exchange Reserves: October 2025 - March 2026
items.summary: It may be recalled that in February 2004 , the Reserve Bank initiated the practice of compiling half-yearly reports on the management of foreign exchange reserves and making them public to promote greater transparency and enhance disclosure in the management of the country’s foreign exchange reserves. In this connection, Reserve Bank of India has today released the 46th Half-Yearly Report on Management of Foreign Exchange Reserves with reference to end-March 2026. During October 2025-March 2026, India’s foreign exchange reserves decreased from USD 700.09 billion to USD 691.11 billion. The late

items.title: Withdrawal of ₹2000 Denomination Banknotes – Status
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) had announced the withdrawal of ₹2000 denomination banknotes from circulation vide Press Release 2023-2024/257 dated May 19, 2023 . The status of withdrawal of ₹2000 banknotes is periodically published by the RBI. The last press release in this regard was published on April 01, 2026 . 2. The facility for deposit and / or exchange of the ₹2000 banknotes was available at all bank branches in the country upto October 07, 2023. 3. The facility for exchange of the ₹2000 banknotes is available at the 19 Issue Offices of the Reserve Bank (RBI Issue Offices) 1 since May

items.title: European markets rebound after Trump threatens Iran
items.summary: European stocks rebounded on Monday as investors react to the latest developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

items.title: Ryanair has plans for 'armageddon' scenario as CFO warns weaker European carriers may not survive jet fuel crunch
items.summary: Ryanair said it has plans for an "armageddon situation," amid the jet fuel crisis.

items.title: Rs 3 a litre fuel price hike cuts losses by 25 per cent; daily loss drops to Rs 750 crore
items.summary: In a noteworthy shift, Indian oil corporations have curtailed daily losses by almost 25% thanks to a Rs 3 per litre increase in petrol and diesel prices. Nevertheless, ongoing global crude oil fluctuation and a depreciating rupee mean that fuel prices still linger below production costs. Currently, there are no discussions surrounding a government financial rescue plan.

items.title: Cognizant doubles 2026 stock buyback target to $2 billion
Meta layoffs starting this week stress harsh AI reality inside Zuckerberg’s company
items.summary: Meta is starting layoffs this week, with 8,000 jobs expected to be cut, as employees brace for a new era of AI.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway has revamped its portfolio — Here's how the new stocks are trading
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway took a $2.6 billion stake in Delta Airlines and increased its shares in Alphabet by 224%.

items.title: Net investments in gold ETFs turn positive after 3 weeks
items.summary: Asians investors turn cautious to book profits, but US, French and UK inflows turn positive

items.title: Jitters on oil and bond markets as US-Iran peace talks stall
items.summary: Energy markets have been on a wild ride as the key Strait of Hormuz waterway remains effectively closed.

items.title: Brent oil tops $110 on report U.S. views latest Iranian proposal as insufficient
items.summary: A senior U.S. official told Axios that Iran's latest proposal is not a meaningful improvement and is insufficient for a deal.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold rebounds $27/oz, silver gains $1/oz as bond yields stabilise
items.summary: Precious metals recovered on 18 May as bond yields stabilised, with gold futures rising by $27 and silver futures by $1.08. Elevated tensions in the Middle East have kept crude oil prices high, while upcoming Federal Reserve meeting minutes may influence the direction of precious metals.

items.title: Captain Polyplast starts production at new Ahmedmad facility
items.summary: Captain Polyplast Limited has commenced operations at its new manufacturing plant near Ahmedabad, significantly boosting its micro-irrigation system production. This facility enables in-house manufacturing of crucial components, promising enhanced cost efficiencies and improved profitability. The company, a prominent player in agricultural equipment, reported strong financial performance in FY25.

items.title: Platinum demand for Indian vehicle production up 1% in Q1, but jewellery offtake down 15%
items.summary: World Platinum Investment Council expects the global market to be in deficit to the tune of 297,000 ounces in 2026, despite surprlus production in the first quarter

items.title: Higher aluminum prices are powering one former Dow component. Here's how to buy for less
items.summary: Despite elevated aluminum production costs and shipment timing issues, Alcoa (AA) is strongly capitalizing on structurally higher prices.

items.title: Proposed look of India’s first ‘Vande Bullet’ train revealed ahead of 2027 launch
Market Trading Guide: Buy PB Fintech, GE Shipping on Tuesday for gains up to 12%
items.summary: Nifty recovered but volatility persists, with resistance at 23,650. Analysts recommend buying PB Fintech (Policybazaar) at Rs 1,749 for an 8% upside and GE Shipping at Rs 1,700 for a 12% upside, citing strong technical breakouts and momentum.

items.title: India should increase investment in R&D to 2 per cent of GDP: Niti Aayog
items.summary: Niti Aayog has urged India to boost research and development investment to two percent of GDP within five years. The report suggests restoring a five percent GST slab for R&D procurement. It also calls for fiscal incentives to encourage private sector R&D spending. Strengthening CSR provisions and tax deductions for R&D donations are also recommended.

items.title: RBI discontinues investment fluctuation reserve requirement for banks
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has discontinued the Investment Fluctuation Reserve for commercial banks. This change takes effect from May 18, 2026. Balances in the IFR will be transferred to other reserves. The Reserve Bank has issued separate circulars for cooperative banks, small finance banks, and payments banks. This move aligns with updated prudential frameworks for market risk and investments.

items.title: IGL Q4 Results: Cons PAT falls 25% YoY to Rs 341 crore, revenue up 6%
items.summary: Indraprastha Gas Limited (IGL) reported a Q4FY26 net profit fall of 25% YoY to Rs 341 crore, driven by an 8% growth in expenses. Despite the profit dip, revenue increased 6% to Rs 4,585 crore. The company board also recommended a final dividend of 75% for the financial year 2025-26.

items.title: The EU will cut growth outlook, raise inflation forecast as Iran war drives 'stagflationary shock'
items.summary: The commission's forthcoming spring forecast, released later this week, will see economic growth figures adjusted down and inflation figures up.

items.title: Is the UK's once favourite car coming back as an EV?
items.summary: The company has announced plans to build seven new models in Europe including a small electric hatchback.

items.title: Royal Enfield plans to set up manufacturing facility in Andhra
items.summary: Royal Enfield is investing Rs 2,500 crore to build a new manufacturing plant in Andhra Pradesh. This expansion will boost production capacity. The company already has significant operations in Tamil Nadu. This move is part of Royal Enfield's strategic growth plans. The new facility will create employment opportunities in Andhra Pradesh. It will support the company's increasing demand.

items.title: As Indian import curbs hit supply, silver to trade at premium
items.summary: Import restrictions on silver spark supply concerns and renewed interest in ETFs

items.title: Today’s Gold Rate in India May 18: Gold prices down in Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Kolkata, Bengaluru, Delhi
items.summary: 22, 24 carat Gold prices down across Indian cities on May 18

items.title: Lululemon takes battle with Chip Wilson public, calls founder 'misguided' and 'outdated'
items.summary: Lululemon responded publicly to founder Chip Wilson's campaign in a letter to shareholders, where it urged them to vote in its favor at its annual meeting.

items.title: SpaceX’s mega-IPO puts a price tag on the fear of missing out
items.summary: Musk’s propensity for futuristic plans has drawn analysts to view Tesla Inc. as a robotics company rather than an automaker and has laid the groundwork for SpaceX to target a $2 trillion valuation when it goes public in the coming weeks

items.title: UPI processes 85% of India's payment volumes but just 9.5% of value; RTGS dominates at 68.6%
items.summary: UPI processed most payment transactions in India by volume in late 2025. RTGS handled significant value with low volume. NEFT offered versatility. Digital payments saw explosive growth from 2021 to 2025. UPI's rise led to a sharp decline in debit card usage for everyday retail payments.

items.title: NextEra Energy to buy Dominion in deal that unites two key players in race to power AI data centers
items.summary: Dominion powers the world's largest data center market in northern Virginia. NextEra is the biggest renewable energy developer in the U.S.

items.title: NextEra Energy strikes $66.8 billion deal for Dominion in bets on AI power demand
items.summary: The deal, one of the largest in the U.S. power industry, adds to a wave of consolidation as the rapid data-center buildout lifts power demand for the first time in two decades, opening up a lucrative revenue stream and boosting profit prospects.

items.title: Offshore India-focused funds see biggest outflows since 2020: Morningstar
items.summary: Foreign investors pulled nearly $5 billion from India-focused offshore funds and ETFs in the March quarter

items.title: UnitedHealth falls after Berkshire sells stake in health insurer
items.summary: UnitedHealth stock ​is up ​about 20% so far this year, after falling more than ⁠30% last year, which had made it the worst performer ​on the Dow Jones Industrial Average index in 2025.

items.title: Tube strikes called off by RMT union
items.summary: The Rail, Maritime and Transport (RMT) union calls off a series of 24-hour strikes starting on Tuesday.

items.title: Pharmacy associations to skip strike, major medicine disruption unlikely
items.summary: India's drug regulator is actively reviewing concerns from retail chemists about e-pharmacies. The All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists had called for a strike. However, many pharmacy associations have stepped back, citing public interest and patient access. The regulator assures that issues are under consideration. Dialogue is seen as key to ensuring uninterrupted healthcare services for citizens.

items.title: Block deal: JSW Energy sells Rs 3,150 crore JSW Steel stake to GQG, SBI Mutual Fund
items.summary: JSW Energy has divested a portion of its JSW Steel stake for Rs 3,150 crore to fund its aggressive power generation expansion. The company aims to reach 30 GW capacity by 2030, bolstered by both renewable and thermal power projects. This strategic move enhances capital allocation and supports long-term growth initiatives.

items.title: Vi pins hope on cashflow growth, loans, tax refunds to meet Rs 1 lakh cr cash requirement
items.summary: Vodafone Idea plans to raise over Rs 1 lakh crore in the next three years. This will fund network upgrades and debt payments. The company expects to triple its cash flow. New loans, tax refunds, and promoter investments will also contribute. This strategy aims to ensure Vi's competitiveness and financial stability in the market.

items.title: Adequate availability of petrol & diesel across the state of Assam: IOCL
items.summary: Fuel supplies in Assam are stable. IndianOil, BPCL, and HPCL confirm adequate stocks of Petrol, Diesel, and LPG. The entire supply chain operates efficiently. Domestic LPG consumers are prioritized. The Oil Industry coordinates logistics and stock movement. Consumers can continue normal consumption. Panic buying is unnecessary.

items.title: GIFT Nifty jumps nearly 1% after reports of US relief on Iran oil sanctions
items.summary: GIFT Nifty rallied nearly 1% after reports indicated possible temporary US sanctions relief on Iranian oil exports. Hopes of additional crude supply eased inflation and energy shock concerns, lifting investor sentiment despite ongoing geopolitical tensions, volatile crude prices, rupee weakness, and uncertainty surrounding West Asia negotiations.

items.title: Broker’s call: Sedemac Mechatronics (Buy)
items.summary: ICICI Securities

items.title: Vodafone Idea draws up ₹1 trillion cash strategy amid dues, capex push
items.summary: Vodafone Idea's plan comes as analysts raise concerns about the company’s future payment obligations to clear spectrum dues, and the company is confident it can raise funds from a State Bank of India-led consortium.

items.title: Rupee may remain under pressure, experts warn of possible slide to 100 per USD
items.summary: Rupee has depreciated by around 6 to 7% against the US dollar this year

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield touches highest in a year, Japan's 30-year yield rises to a record
items.summary: Global bond yields rose on Monday as fears of resurgent inflation grip financial markets.

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks trade mixed as Treasury yields ease, oil prices retreat
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mixed in choppy trade as Treasury yields eased from recent highs and oil prices declined on easing supply concerns. Investors remained cautious amid inflation worries and rate hike bets, while AI-driven optimism and key earnings kept markets supported.

items.title: Not activating countercyclical capital buffer, says RBI
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has decided not to activate the countercyclical capital buffer. This measure is not needed at this time. The buffer is designed to build capital in good times for use during difficult periods. It also aims to curb excessive lending. The framework was introduced after the 2008 global financial crisis.

items.title: Haryana cabinet approves new rules: No petrol, diesel vehicles for cab aggregators in NCR
items.summary: Haryana's Cabinet has approved new rules for vehicle aggregators. From January 1, 2026, all new vehicles joining fleets in NCR areas must be electric, CNG, or use cleaner fuels. This aims to reduce pollution and promote green mobility. The rules also introduce licensing, safety measures, and insurance for drivers and passengers.

items.title: SBI employees' federation stages dharna in Kolkata, plans nationwide strike on May 25-26
items.summary: State Bank of India Staff Federation protested in Kolkata demanding recruitment and an end to mis-selling insurance. Employees will strike nationwide on May 25 and 26. The federation alleges issues with resolving employee concerns and agreements. Previous protests included badge-wearing and demonstrations. Future actions involve candle marches and the planned strike.

items.title: Multibagger Apollo Micro Systems stock jumps over 5% after strong Q4 results; rebounds 70% in under 2 months
items.summary: Apollo Micro Systems saw a 5.6% rise to ₹311 as investors reacted positively to its strong March quarter results, reporting an 81.3% YoY revenue increase to ₹293.26 crore and a 163.5% jump in PAT to ₹36.79 crore.

items.title: UK plans to relax key banking rules set up after 2008 financial crisis
items.summary: Chief secretary to the Treasury says softening ringfencing regulations would free up £80bn for extra lending

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: Apollo Micro & Zydus Wellness Q4 profit up, Strides Pharma profit down q-o-q, GNFC Q4 PAT up 88%, IOC, JSW Cement, DOMS Industries, GE Vernova, Astral, Afcons Infra to announce Q4 results
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, 18th May 2026 Live Updates: Catch live updates

items.title: RBI imposes restrictions on Etawah-based Nagar Sahakari Bank for 6 months
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has placed restrictions on Etawah-based Nagar Sahakari Bank. Customers can withdraw a maximum of Rs 10,000. The bank cannot grant new loans or accept fresh deposits without RBI approval. These measures are in place for six months to protect depositors. The RBI will monitor the bank's financial health and adjust the directions as needed.

items.title: 7 large-cap stocks post highest quarterly profits in 10 quarters
items.summary: Several NSE large-cap companies reported record March 2026 quarterly profits, surpassing previous ten-quarter highs by over 25%. Strong operational performance, improved fundamentals, and favorable market conditions boosted earnings for firms including ABB India, JSW Steel, ICICI Bank, Bajaj Finance, Tata Motors, and Muthoot Finance.

items.title: UK growth forecast upgraded by IMF but risks remain
items.summary: Growth has been upgraded from 0.8% to 1% for 2026 in the influential body's latest forecast.

items.title: Current account deficit to widen to 2.3 per cent of GDP in FY27 from 0.9 pc in FY26: Report
items.summary: India's current account deficit is expected to rise to 2.3 percent of GDP in FY27 from 0.9 percent in FY26. This widening deficit could strain foreign exchange reserves. Experts suggest policy changes like increasing fuel prices and operationalizing trade deals to boost inflows. Attracting sustainable capital is crucial for India's economic stability.

items.title: India unveils official glimpse of its first ever bullet train
items.summary: India's first bullet train for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad corridor is set to transform travel. An image of the high-speed train has been unveiled at the Ministry of Railways. This 508-km link will connect Mumbai to Ahmedabad, reaching speeds of 320 km/h.

items.title: Flipkart Pulls Ahead: How India's E-Commerce Giant is Winning Hearts and Carts
items.summary: From weekly active users to GMV share, two new institutional reports reveal that Flipkart is extending its lead over competitors and building a multi-business moat that rivals struggle to replicate.

items.title: IDFC FIRST Bank Q4 shows deposit slowdown despite system-wide surge
items.summary: Despite a system-wide rise in bank deposits, IDFC FIRST Bank’s March-quarter earnings showed a sequential slowdown in deposits after the Chandigarh fraud dented depositor confidence.

items.title: Imagicaaworld to invest ₹100 crore in Gujarat water park as footfalls rise 5%
items.summary: The announcement comes alongside the company’s Q4FY26 results, which showed revenue from operations of ₹91.9 crore; Footfalls across its parks rose 5 per cent year-on-year to 6.21 lakh visitors, signalling steady consumer demand

items.title: Quote of the day by Meir Statman: "The market may be crazy, but that doesn’t make you a psychiatrist"
items.summary: Behavioral finance expert Meir Statman advises investors against trying to diagnose market volatility, emphasizing that the market's irrationality doesn't require an investor's interpretation. Instead of attempting to predict every twist and turn, focusing on disciplined investing, fundamentals, and risk management proves more effective for long-term success.

items.title: Trump says he should've asked for 'more' of Intel when negotiating stake with CEO
items.summary: The chipmaker's stock has soared since the U.S. equity deal in August gave the government 9.9% of the company.

items.title: FIFA media rights officials visit India where no World Cup broadcast deal is yet signed
items.summary: FIFA officials are in India this week to secure a broadcast deal for the upcoming World Cup. Millions of Indian football fans may miss the tournament due to a deadlock over broadcast rights. A previous offer from Reliance-Disney was significantly lower than FIFA's asking price.

items.title: Honda City facelift reaches dealerships ahead of May 22 launch; discounts rain on old model
items.summary: The Honda City facelift has been spotted at dealerships ahead of its May 22 India launch, showcasing cosmetic and feature upgrades. Buyers can still avail significant discounts on the current model. The updated sedan is expected to feature revised styling, a larger infotainment screen, ventilated seats, and a 360-degree camera, while retaining its current engine options.

items.title: Indian markets capable of absorbing different types of shocks: SEBI chief on West Asia crisis
items.summary: "Due to the prevailing conflict in West Asia, the oil supply chain and its prices got affected in the rest of the world. All the economies have been affected by this and obviously, there are inflationary risks. Besides, spillover effect and second-order effect will also come in," he said.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Dow futures fall up to 0.8% as crude prices stay elevated; Brent hits $112
items.summary: US stock futures declined as investors awaited Nvidia's earnings, with oil prices rising amid the US-Iran stalemate. Major indices dropped over 1% in the previous trade as tech stocks faced pressure. The unresolved conflict poses risks to the AI-driven market surge and overall economic stability.

items.title: Arohan Financial Services files IPO papers with Sebi; eyes Rs 600 cr via fresh issue
items.summary: US Teachers Insurance and Annuity Association, a retirement financial services firm, Michael & Susan Dell Foundation, Aavishkaar Goodwell India Microfinance Development Company-II Ltd, Tano Capital, TR Capital III Mauritius, and Danish Sustainable Development Goals Investment Fund, among other existing investors, will offload shares.

items.title: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stocks trade mixed as Treasury yields ease, oil prices retreat
INDIA BONDS-Oil-led global bond rout pushes India 10-year yield to 7-week high
items.summary: Indian bonds have increasingly tracked U.S. Treasury moves in recent ​years, as higher U.S. yields narrow the return ​premium on emerging-market debt, spur foreign outflows and pressure the rupee.

items.title: Indian firms are turning to floating-rate bonds as interest rate hikes loom. Here’s why
items.summary: Some firms are unable to raise planned funds via fixed-rate bonds, traders say

items.title: Indian firms turn to floating-rate debt as interest rate hikes loom
items.summary: Coupons for floating-rate bonds are priced at a spread over three-month Treasury bill yields and reset quarterly. When rate hikes are expected, these bonds turn more ‌attractive to both ⁠issuers and ⁠investors - companies can borrow at a lower initial cost, while investors benefit from returns that rise over time.

items.title: Nifty claws back from 23,317 abyss; Rupee hits record low near ₹96.40
items.summary: Nifty 50 settled at 23,649.95, up just 6.45 points or 0.03%, while the BSE Sensex gained 77.05 points or 0.10% to close at 75,315.04

items.title: ‘This is bad’: Strategists see European oil shortages within weeks as inventories are depleted
items.summary: The Iran war oil squeeze could trigger shortages in Europe within weeks, and keep global inventories depleted until 2027, strategists warn.

items.title: DLF-GIC JV firm's rent income rises 16% to Rs 5,525 cr in FY26 on demand for commercial spaces
items.summary: DCCDL, a joint venture between DLF and GIC, saw its rental income climb 16 percent to Rs 5,525 crore last fiscal. This growth was driven by strong demand for its prime office and retail assets. The company's office rents increased by 17 percent, while retail rents grew by 11 percent. DCCDL's total operational portfolio spans 44.3 million square feet.

items.title: Gland Pharma,6 stocks hit 52-week highs, rally up to 20% in a month
US stocks to buy for short term: From MP Materials to Arista Networks- Appreciate CEO suggests picking these 5 shares
items.summary: Subho Moulik identifies five US stocks poised for short-term success, including MP Materials and Eaton. With AI capital expenditure soaring, these companies are positioned to benefit from unprecedented demand in the tech landscape.

items.title: Mangalam Worldwide approves 10-for-1 stock split, eyes BSE main board listing
items.summary: The moves come on the back of MWL’s financial results for FY26, in which the company reported revenue of ₹1,214.98 crore and profit after tax of ₹50.14 crore. The board has also recommended a final dividend of ₹0.30 per share for FY26

items.title: Gainers & Losers: Cochin Shipyard, Suven Life Sciences among 6 top movers on Monday
Broker’s call: Data Patterns (Add)
items.summary: Choice International

items.title: NSE kicks off trading in Electronic Gold Receipts: Is it a better way to invest than gold ETFs and physical metal?
items.summary: Since each of the EGRs is backed by physical gold, the investors, at their discretion, can surrender the EGRs and take physical delivery of the corresponding quantity and quality of gold.

items.title: India's fertiliser subsidy bill for FY27 may rise by Rs 70,000 cr on West Asia crisis, official says
items.summary: India's fertiliser subsidy bill could rise by Rs 70,000 crore to Rs 2.41 lakh crore in 2026-27. This increase is driven by higher import costs for urea and other fertilisers due to the West Asia crisis. Despite cost pressures, fertiliser availability for the 2026 kharif season remains comfortable. Diversified import sourcing is bridging any supply gaps.

items.title: Cruel summer: How Iran war shocks are silently gutting corporate profits
items.summary: The West Asian geopolitical shock has ignited an industrial wildfire in India. From Morbi’s kilns to Tiruppur’s factories, a hidden crisis of soaring costs and fleeing workers is quietly triggering margin erosion—and the worst may be yet to come.

items.title: Finance ministry asks banks, insurance companies for cost cutting, move to EVs
items.summary: The finance ministry has directed all public sector banks, insurers, and financial institutions to switch to electric vehicles, minimise foreign travel by top executives, and move all meetings to video conferencing with immediate effect.

items.title: Surging global bond yields, elevated oil deepen rupee's slide to record lows
items.summary: The currency has slumped 2% over the last seven trading sessions. ​Traders said the losses would have been steeper if not for market interventions by the ​Reserve Bank of India.

items.title: Anarock Group FY26 revenue rises 25% to Rs 946 cr
items.summary: The remaining revenue came from facilitating the sale as well as leasing of office, retail and warehousing spaces; strategic consulting; project management; co-working platform, consultancy in the hospitality segment; and society management mobile application.

items.title: Liberty Mutual Insurance raises stake in Indian arm to 74 pc
items.summary: Liberty Mutual Insurance has increased its stake in Liberty General Insurance to 74%, strengthening its commitment to India’s fast-growing insurance market. Following an earlier stake hike in September 2025, the company plans to expand distribution networks and deepen its presence across retail and commercial insurance segments, while capitalising on rising insurance penetration opportunities in India.

items.title: Global bond rout deepens as Iran war drags on and underscores inflation fears
items.summary: Global bond markets are experiencing significant losses as rising energy prices, fueled by the Iran conflict, ignite inflation fears. Investors now anticipate central banks worldwide, including the US Federal Reserve and European Central Bank, to hike interest rates. Japan's government debt issuance further strains finances, pushing JGB yields to record highs, while Eurozone yields also climb.

items.title: Weight-loss wars go global as Novo Nordisk bets big on its Wegovy pill
items.summary: The weight loss pill had a strong U.S. launch this year, and Novo believes that will be replicated in other markets around the world.

items.title: IEA chief warns commercial oil inventories are depleting rapidly, only weeks left
items.summary: International Energy Agency head Fatih Birol warned that commercial oil inventories are rapidly depleting, with only a few weeks of supply remaining due to the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure. Strategic reserve releases have provided temporary relief, but demand is increasing with seasonal shifts. The agency previously forecast a surplus but now anticipates a significant shortfall this year.

items.title: Inside India’s Next-Gen Workspaces: The Rise of Intelligent Commercial Ecosystems
items.summary: L&T Realty is expanding its commercial real estate footprint while setting new benchmarks for India’s modern work environment

items.title: SAIL shares volatile after Q4 earnings, brokerages divided on steel cycle outlook
items.summary: The stock declined nearly 2 per cent to ₹188.21 in early trade before rebounding to an intraday high of ₹193.99 against the previous close of ₹192.40. The stock later traded at ₹190.02 on the NSE at 10.57 am.

items.title: Delhivery shares slide 4% after Q4 results despite strong revenue growth
items.summary: The stock fell after the March quarter earnings announcement, as investors reacted to lower profitability and integration costs, despite robust growth in express parcel volumes and transportation

items.title: India buying Russian oil irrespective of US sanctions waivers, Indian official says
items.summary: India has consistently purchased Russian oil, irrespective of U.S. sanctions waivers, according to a petroleum ministry official. The decision is driven by commercial sense, with ample crude supply secured. This purchasing strategy remains unaffected by any waiver status.

items.title: Tata Steel shares down 3% despite strong Q4 earnings, brokerages divided on outlook
items.summary: Tata Steel posted over two-fold jump in consolidated net profit for the March quarter at ₹2,965 cr.

items.title: PRO FX revenue jumps 37% to Rs 177 crore in FY26, profit rises 24% on premium AV demand
items.summary: PRO FX Tech saw significant growth in FY26. Revenue jumped 37% to Rs 177 crore, and net profit rose 24% to Rs 15 crore. This success was fueled by strong demand for premium audio products and effective operations. The company navigated economic challenges through cost management and a focus on high-value customers.

items.title: China's economy loses steam in April as retail sales hit 40-month low
items.summary: China's economy stumbled in April with consumption, industrial output and investment growth missing expectations.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex closes flat, Nifty ends at 23,650 as Iran war worries offset IT gains
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: India's equity benchmarks closed flat on ​Monday as persistent concerns over the impact of the ‌Iran war offset a partial rebound in IT stocks.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why Swiggy wants to be classified as Indian-controlled
items.summary: Swiggy’s proposed governance changes highlight how Indian startups with large foreign investors are restructuring boards and shareholder rights to navigate tighter e-commerce and quick commerce rules.

items.title: 'Track imports, manufacture in India': Piyush Goyal pushes swadeshi production
items.summary: Indian businesses are encouraged to monitor imports and identify opportunities for local manufacturing. This push for self-reliance aims to strengthen domestic industries. India's exports are growing, with ambitious targets set for the coming years. Free trade agreements are being expanded to enhance market access. A Swadeshi trade fair will showcase Indian products and services, promoting 'Made in India' goods.

items.title: Rupee hits fresh low of 96.34 per US dollar amid surging global bond yields, elevated oil
items.summary: The currency has slumped 2% over the last seven trading sessions. Traders said ‌the losses would have been steeper if not for ‌market ⁠interventions by the Reserve Bank of India.

items.title: Commercial oil inventories depleting rapidly, only weeks left: IEA chief Birol
items.summary: Release of strategic oil reserves had added 2.5 million barrels ‌of oil per day ​to the market, he said

items.title: Markets rebound sharply: Sensex recovers 1,100 points from day’s low, Nifty closes near 23,650
items.summary: Sensex rose 1,100 points from its day’s low to close at 75,315, and Nifty 50 gained 333 points from its day’s low to end the session at 23,650. At close, Sensex was up 77 points and Nifty was up 6 points from Friday’s closing levels.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on May 18: Gland Pharma, Coforge, PB Fintech, BSE, Voltas, Bharti Airtel among top gainers
items.summary: Indian stocks marginally gained on May 18, driven by strong tech buying and pharma support, with Nifty 50 up 0.03% at 23,649 and BSE Sensex at 75,263. However, the broader market faced pressure as Nifty Midcap 100 and Nifty Smallcap 100 fell.

items.title: India's cooking fuel shortage is driving up California's gas prices
items.summary: India's cooking gas shortage, driven by the Strait of Hormuz closure, forces refiners to cut alkylate production. This directly impacts California's gasoline supply, as alkylates are crucial for its unique blend, leading to higher prices for motorists already facing a global fuel crisis.

items.title: SEBI working on framework for AI-based trading amid growing cyber threats: Tuhin Kanta Pandey
items.summary: Pandey says AI is helping financial entities automate processes and expand multilingual investor outreach, but warns that it also amplifies vulnerabilities that could threaten market integrity

items.title: HS2 failings blamed on high-speed focus and political pressure
items.summary: A new reviews confirms the high-speed rail line's "original sins" include a technical design, changing political priorities and ballooning costs.

items.title: AIASL employees' protest at Mumbai airport leads to delay in Air India, AI Express flights
items.summary: Mumbai airport witnessed significant flight delays on Monday as AIASL ground handling staff staged a protest for better wages. Air India and Air India Express operations were affected. The employees resumed work after management assured them their demands would be considered. AIASL, a major ground handler, serves numerous airlines across India.

items.title: Mangalam Worldwide’s board approves 1:10 stock split as smallcap rises 120% in a year
items.summary: Mangalam Worldwide has approved a 1:10 stock split, under which each equity share with a face value of Rs 10 will be subdivided into 10 shares of Re 1 each, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals. The decision was cleared by the board on May 15, 2026.

items.title: European oil refiners and airlines have ‘almost zero’ jet fuel shortage concerns
items.summary: Refiners maximise production and increase imports from US and Africa to offset loss of Middle Eastern supplies

items.title: Stock market today: Sensex ends over 1,100 points higher from day's low; Nifty 50 near 23,650
items.summary: The Sensex closed 77 points, or 0.10%, higher at 75,315.04, while the Nifty 50 settled at 23,649.95, up 6 points, or 0.03%.

items.title: Retail investing has surged in India, but rural participation remains low, says Sebi Chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey
items.summary: India's securities markets have seen substantial growth. Retail investor numbers have surged, and market capitalization has climbed significantly. However, actual investment participation lags behind awareness. SEBI is enhancing investor protection and launching initiatives to boost financial literacy. Disciplined long-term investing is encouraged for financial empowerment.

items.title: India Inc's earnings growth to slow down in next 12-18 months, warns Moody’s Ratings. Here’s why
items.summary: Moody’s Ratings warns that India’s corporate earnings growth may slow over the next 12–18 months due to rising input costs, rupee depreciation, supply-chain disruptions, and labour market uncertainty. The agency also flagged weaker consumption, delayed investments, and sectoral pressures across autos, airlines, metals, and oil marketing companies amid global risks.

items.title: FPIs sell over ₹2 lakh crore in Indian stock market so far in 2026. Are they driving the boom-and-bust cycle?
items.summary: In 2026, FPIs have aggressively sold over ₹2.19 lakh crore in Indian equities, surpassing 2025 outflows. This has weakened the rupee significantly, with total FPI ownership now at 15%. Domestic investors are absorbing some pressure, but concerns over global capital flight persist.

items.title: Airtel dethrones HDFC Bank as India’s second-most valuable company
items.summary: Airtel’s total market cap stood at ₹11,80,328 crore, narrowly edging past HDFC Bank’s ₹11,82,223 crore — a gap that fluctuated through the session after Airtel hit an intraday high of ₹1,953.80.

items.title: NTPC Group's installed electricity generation capacity crosses 90GW mark
items.summary: State-owned NTPC has crossed a significant milestone, achieving over 90 GW of installed electricity generation capacity. This expansion was driven by the successful trial operation of Unit 2 at its Patratu Vidyut Utpadan Nigam arm. The company is actively pursuing renewable energy targets and diversifying its energy portfolio.

items.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50: Promoter-backed DII holds stake in tech stock in Q4FY26 despite selloff on D-Street
items.summary: On Monday, Kellton Tech share price fell by over 3% on NSE, touching an intraday low of ₹14.49 apiece. According to data available on NSE, Matnic Finvest has maintained its stake at 32.97% in the March quarter (Q4FY26).

items.title: From train travel to staycations: How holidaymakers are adapting to airlines' jet fuel shortage
items.summary: "I think people will increasingly decide, let's book Portugal, Spain, Italy... let's avoid the Middle East or going long haul," Ryanair's CEO told CNBC.

items.title: MCX to facilitate delivery of India refined silver
items.summary: Move comes on the back of Government decision to curb import of gold and silver to bridge the current account deficit.

items.title: Hind Rectifiers shares slide 3.5% as strong FY26 revenue masks margin pressure
items.summary: The stock touched an intraday low of ₹797.55 before recovering, and trades at a P/E of 84.28 — well above the broader market.

items.title: EPACK Prefab posts 34.5% revenue jump in FY26, stock falls 4%
items.summary: The stock traded at ₹192.41 on the NSE, down 4% from ₹200.42, with sell orders making up over 80% of the total traded quantity

items.title: Coforge to Tech Mahindra: Tech stocks extend rally for second session; Nifty IT jumps over 2%
items.summary: Coforge, Tech Mahindra, and other tech stocks rallied on May 18, with the NIFTY IT index rising 2.4%. Analysts attribute this to currency weakness and export earnings, despite concerns over AI disruption and mixed earnings guidance from major firms, leading to a decline of 25.3% in 2026.

items.title: FIIs trim holdings in 10 midcap stocks over two quarters; shares drop up to 50%
items.summary: FII selling, midcap stocks, falling FII stake, foreign investor exit, stock market trends, Kaynes Technology, KPIT Technologies, Thermax shares, Bandhan Bank, midcap selloff

items.title: German 10-year bond yield stays at 15-year high amid global bond rout
items.summary: Global bond markets are experiencing a selloff. Investors are concerned about rising energy prices due to the Middle East conflict. This could fuel inflation and lead to further interest rate hikes. German 10-year bond yields have reached a 15-year high. Italian 10-year yields are also up. European Central Bank head Christine Lagarde acknowledged concerns.

items.title: Instant AI answers can trivialise human intelligence, warns Royal Observatory
items.summary: Paddy Rodgers said the Observatory's rich history showed the power of human knowledge and the need to avoid "dependence" on AI.

items.title: Oswal Pumps shares slide, posted record FY26 earnings
items.summary: The stock, listed in June 2025, has lost roughly 29% year-to-date and trades at less than half its 52-week high of ₹888.40 hit in August 2025

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit 2026: Decoding the future of Indian stock market in uncertain times
items.summary: Indian investors navigate a complex wealth creation landscape. Geopolitical shifts and rapid AI adoption demand new strategies. The ET Alpha Wealth Summit 2026 on June 4 in Mumbai will gather top minds. Discussions will focus on AI, private markets, and global capital flows. Learn how to build resilient portfolios and identify future opportunities.

items.title: Table Space adds 5 lakh sq ft in Hyderabad amid rising GCC demand
items.summary: Table Space has significantly expanded its Hyderabad presence by adding nearly 500,000 square feet of workspace. This growth is fueled by increasing demand from global capability centers and various enterprises. The company's largest deal is at Grava Business Park in Kokapet. This expansion strengthens Hyderabad's position as a key office and GCC hub in India.

items.title: Commodity Radar: Rupee's record lows support gold prices. Can fresh base formation trigger upside?
items.summary: Gold futures on MCX are holding above key support levels as rupee weakness nears record lows, boosting domestic prices despite global softness. Analysts say MCX gold is showing early signs of base formation, with consolidation above Rs 1.58 lakh potentially paving the way for further upside if momentum sustains.

items.title: Gold loan giant Muthoot FinCorp plans Rs 4,000 crore IPO. Check details
items.summary: Muthoot FinCorp has approved plans to raise up to Rs 4,000 crore via an IPO, aiming to fund expansion in the fast-growing gold loan market. The company, fully owned by its promoter family, plans a fresh issue of shares as strong gold prices and demand boost sector growth prospects.

items.title: Indian bond yields spike to six-week high as crude oil price rally jolts markets
items.summary: Bond markets from Tokyo to New York came under pressure following fresh attacks in the Gulf region. The yield on the benchmark 10-year US Treasury note surged to a 15-month high of 4.6310%.

items.title: Gold futures rise ₹598 in volatile trade amid tensions in West Asia
items.summary: On the Multi Commodity Exchange, the yellow metal for the June delivery appreciated by ₹598, or 0.38% to ₹1,59,145 per 10 grams

items.title: India's hotel sector attracts USD 185 mn investment in Jan-Mar, jumps 58 pc: JLL
items.summary: Investments in India's hotel sector saw a significant jump. In the first quarter of 2026, investments rose 58 percent to USD 185 million. This reflects strong investor confidence in the hotel business's growth potential. Last year, investments had already surged 67 percent. This upward trend continues, driven by ample liquidity and new opportunities.

items.title: Strong Q4 rural recovery runs into rising cost and monsoon risks
items.summary: Consumption-linked companies posted their strongest sales growth in years in Q4FY26. Analysts now warn that crude-led inflation and monsoon risks could choke demand in H1FY27.

items.title: JSW Energy sells 2.5 crore JSW Steel shares for Rs 3,150 cr via bulk deal
items.summary: JSW Energy on Monday said it sold 2.5 crore equity shares in JSW Steel through a bulk deal on the NSE, raising Rs 3,150 crore. Following the transaction, JSW Energy’s remaining holding in JSW Steel stands at 4.5 crore equity shares, according to a regulatory filing.

items.title: Britain’s prospective next PM tries to placate bond markets after sell-off, gilts steady
items.summary: The U.K.'s would-be prime minister, Andy Burnham, is being closely watched by bond markets.

items.title: Old shopping funnel is gone: Gen Z is rewriting rules of retail
items.summary: Gen Z is rapidly reshaping India’s online retail market, forcing brands and platforms to rethink traditional marketing strategies. Nearly half of India’s online fashion and beauty shoppers now belong to Gen Z, with companies like Amazon, Nykaa, Myntra and Shein tailoring their offerings to meet the cohort’s fast-changing preferences and high engagement levels.

items.title: Tata Steel’s strong quarter meets a Dutch roadblock
items.summary: Shares fall 4% despite solid Q4FY26 as possible closure of coke and gas plants in the Netherlands raises margin risks; pricing gains and volume growth offer partial cushion.

items.title: Amazon Now to deploy 1,000 Eicher electric trucks
items.summary: Amazon India is introducing 1,000 electric trucks for its Amazon Now service by 2028. Fifty trucks are already on the road. These vehicles will transport goods between fulfillment centers and micro-fulfillment centers. The initiative aims to support quick commerce operations across India, starting with key cities.

items.title: TCS asks managers to classify 5% staff as underperformers
items.summary: Internal emails show TCS pushing managers to place 5% of employees in the lowest performance band, stoking fears of further workforce cuts after last year’s retrenchment exercise.

items.title: India can ride out disruption after Russian oil waiver lapses
items.summary: India's oil refiners are prepared for changes in Russian crude supply. Weaker domestic demand and available oil from the US and Middle East offer alternatives. China's reduced market presence also helps maintain supply. Maintenance at major refiners has increased Russian crude in storage. This provides Indian refiners with more options in the short term.

items.title: Bonds extend sell-off on inflation fears
items.summary: US and Japanese yields rise amid investor concern over surge in energy prices

items.title: US-Iran war: Why PM Modi visited UAE amid Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz crisis
items.summary: Amid escalating tensions from the US-Iran war and soaring oil prices, India's Prime Minister Modi's recent visit to the UAE aims to secure energy supplies and foster deeper economic ties. With the UAE's strategic shift in oil policy, India seeks to diversify imports and enhance energy security.

items.title: El Nino spells trouble for kharif harvests across India
items.summary: A study by scientists at an ICAR institute has found that El Niño events have consistently reduced yields of major kharif crops in India, with significant impacts across multiple states. Research led by Subash N Pillai at the ICAR-Indian Institute of Farming Systems Research shows that paddy output fell by over 10% in 77 districts, while maize production declined similarly in 65 districts during El Niño years.

items.title: Why Apollo Tyres’ strong quarter may not mark a turning point
items.summary: The management indicated that input costs could rise in the high-teens in Q1FY27, driven primarily by elevated natural rubber prices. Further, new vehicle purchases would be impacted if fuel/commodity costs continue their inflationary trajectory.

items.title: EXIM Bank eyes 10% jump in loan book in FY27 despite West Asia tensions
items.summary: Export-Import Bank of India expects its loan book to grow 10% in FY27 despite geopolitical tensions in West Asia, according to Managing Director and CEO Harsha Bangari. The projected growth is lower than FY26’s 12% rise, which was partly boosted by favourable currency movements as a large share of the bank’s loans are denominated in foreign currencies.

items.title: Asia-Pacific markets mostly fall as Trump’s Iran warning stokes fresh oil supply fears
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets fell as investors weighed renewed geopolitical tensions after Donald Trump warned Iran to "get moving, FAST."

items.title: Why is Vodafone Idea share price falling despite a surprise ₹51,970-crore profit in Q4? Explained
items.summary: Vodafone Idea share price opened at ₹12.81 today as against the last closing price of ₹12.96 and dropped to an intraday low of ₹12.45 soon thereafter, shedding 3.93%. The company had posted its Q4 results on Saturday.

items.title: AI, not engineers for coding: Salesforce to spend $300 million on Anthropic tokens, CEO Marc Benioff says
items.summary: Benioff's plans, coupled with his previous announcement that Salesforce will not hire any more engineers in 2025 due to productivity gains from AI, signal a decisive shift — the automation of writing code.

items.title: NFP Sampoorna Foods IPO Day 1: Issue booked 20% so far. Check GMP, subscription status, key details to know
items.summary: NFP Sampoorna Foods IPO opened on 18 May, priced between ₹52 to ₹55 per share. The company specialises in dry fruits, particularly cashew nuts, and aims to raise ₹24.53 crore through this offering. Subscription status shows 20% on day 1.

items.title: US tech boom isn’t a dotcom bubble, but valuations warrant caution: Viram Shah of Vested Finance
items.summary: The Magnificent Seven now account for roughly 35% of the S&P 500’s market capitalisation, the highest concentration in modern history. The CAPE ratio is close to 40 — near dotcom-era levels — while the Buffett Indicator stands at about 230% of GDP.

items.title: L Catterton said to be in talks to acquire minority stake in Bengaluru-based Nandhana Palace
items.summary: Private equity firm L Catterton is in discussions to acquire a stake in Nandhana Palace, which is expanding its restaurant chain across India amidst increasing investor interest in the food services sector.

items.summary: The internet giant dwarfs other online retailers on both sides of the Atlantic.

items.summary: Indian equity markets experienced a significant selloff on May 18, with Sensex and Nifty 50 dropping over 1%. The BSE Sensex fell over 1,000 points, erasing nearly ₹9 lakh crore in investor wealth amid weak global cues and a bearish trend across sectors.

items.title: SBI Report: No direct impact of oil price hike on India's fiscal situation
items.summary: Fuel prices have risen by Rs 3 per litre. This move aims to reduce losses for oil companies. SBI's research report Ecowrap suggests that the impact on consumer inflation will be temporary. Annual fuel consumption is expected to remain stable. The government's fiscal situation is not directly affected by this price adjustment. State governments may see revenue changes if excise duties are altered.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: G7 to assess economic shock
items.summary: The Group of 7 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors are concerned by higher borrowing costs and debt as they meet in Paris.

items.summary: Bharti Airtel has become the second-largest firm by market capitalisation, overtaking HDFC Bank. Analysts predict further growth as the stock breaks through previous resistance levels, with expectations to hit ₹2,100 soon.

items.title: Bigger than Tesla, Meta, Berkshire: Meet the $2-trillion tech giant most people barely know
items.summary: Broadcom stock price has delivered more than 28% over the past six months and surged nearly 85% in the last year. Over five years, Broadcom share price has skyrocketed by about 875%.

items.summary: Cupid share price rose 3.4% on May 18 after announcing strong Q4 FY26 results, with a 26% year-over-year income increase. The company plans aggressive growth, targeting ₹600 crore revenue in FY27 and expanding its FMCG distribution and product portfolio.

items.summary: In the trade on Monday, 18 May, the Sensex crashed over 1000 points, and the Nifty 50 slipped to near 27,300 levels as both indices lost another 1% amid no end in sight to the conflict in West Asia, which is beginning to challenge global growth and drive inflation.

items.summary: South Korea’s government stepped up pressure on Samsung Electronics and its union to avert a strike that officials say could cost the economy billions.

items.summary: Morgan Stanley sees India's economy entering a new growth phase. Strong domestic demand and policy support are expected to boost corporate earnings. Indian equities are set for a strong year. Investments in key sectors will strengthen growth momentum. India's role in global GDP growth is increasing.

items.summary: While the Madhya Pradesh government projects it as a major reform to streamline fertiliser distribution, industry stakeholders say it’s still too early to assess.

items.title: PM Modi woos Swedish cos to invest in India, says 'reform express' going at full speed
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged top European companies to increase investments in India during a high-level meeting with the European Round Table for Industry in Gothenburg, Sweden. The event was co-hosted by Swedish Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen.

items.title: Centre plans special FASTags for toll-exempt vehicles under AI highway tolling
items.summary: The government is considering dedicated FASTags and integrated vehicle databases to prevent exempt defence and official vehicles from being wrongly penalised under the MLFF tolling system.

items.title: Gautam Adani is overhauling his conglomerate, from Apple-style vendors to less hierarchy and more talent grooming
items.summary: The ports-to-renewable energy conglomerate is aggressively developing a third-party vendor ecosystem to take over manufacturing, logistics, engineering and other functions.

items.title: Rapido has innovated, but we've innovated faster: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi
items.summary: Dara Khosrowshahi said Uber will continue investing aggressively in India despite losses, betting on mobility, EVs and bike taxis. He discussed competition from Rapido, robotaxis, regulation and Uber’s long-term India strategy.

items.title: India plans Rs 35,000 crore highway asset monetisation in FY27
items.summary: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has prepared a list of the highway stretches for monetisation with Haryana accounting for the highest number of assets followed by Uttar Pradesh.

items.title: As IPO momentum fades, VCs embrace partial exits and M&A
items.summary: Venture capital investors are stepping back from IPOs as the primary exit route, turning instead to secondary transactions, founder buybacks and M&A as public market conditions worsen.

items.title: With largest US commuter rail system shut down, federal labor officials urge unions to resume talks
items.summary: With largest US commuter rail system shut down, federal labor officials urge unions to resume talks

items.title: Jane Street Doubles Capacity in Singapore as Expansion Rolls On
items.summary: Jane Street Group LLC moved into a new Singapore office this month, doubling its seating capacity to 250 people as it continues to ramp up operations across Asia, said a person with knowledge of the matter.

items.title: At Samsung, the global AI boom spurred a looming strike and deep divisions
items.summary: SAMSUNG-ELEC-DISPUTE:At Samsung, the global AI boom spurred a looming strike and deep divisions

items.title: ET Graphics: BoP stability tested by outflows & Rupee fall
items.summary: India's external accounts are under strain. The current account deficit is widening, and capital may flow out. The rupee has weakened significantly. Policymakers are considering various measures beyond interest rate hikes to stabilize the currency. These include incentives for foreign deposits and liberalizing capital inflows. State-owned banks may also issue foreign currency bonds.

items.title: RBI tightens scrutiny of overseas investments as outflows surge to $27 billion in FY26
items.summary: Regulators are examining overseas investments by Indian companies. The Reserve Bank of India is seeking details on the purpose and structure of these foreign ventures. This scrutiny follows a significant increase in overseas direct investment outflows. Companies must demonstrate genuine business intent for these investments. The RBI aims to ensure funds are used appropriately and profits are repatriated.

items.title: Exports diversification drive adds $202 million to kitty in FY26
items.summary: India's exports expanded significantly last fiscal year. New product and country combinations generated over $200 million in new trade. Ships, telecom instruments, and agri-products saw growth in new markets. This diversification shows a shift towards higher-value manufacturing and technology. Exports to Northeast Asia and Latin America also surged, indicating growing global demand for Indian goods.

items.title: Trump warns Iran to 'get moving' or 'there won’t be anything left'
items.summary: The president has previously threatened to strike civilian infrastructure in Iran, a potential war crime.

items.title: The looming energy crunch
items.summary: Supply squeeze due to the Iran war will get worse before it gets better

items.title: Tipping point looms for global energy crisis
items.summary: Emergency measures spread to nearly 80 countries as oil stockpiles run low on back of Middle East conflict

items.title: Prudential Plc to buy 75% Bharti Life stake for ₹3,500 crore, cut ICICI Pru Life holding to 10%
items.summary: The London-based insurer says the move marks a strategic reset in India, as it secures majority control of Bharti Life Insurance while complying with regulations on ICICI Prudential stake reduction.

items.title: JSW Steel’s JVs put it on track to be among top global steelmakers outside China
items.summary: Including planned capacity expansions at BPSL, the tie-ups with Japan's JFE Steel and Korea's POSCO would add about 16 mtpa capacity by 2032, setting it on course to reach 80 mtpa capacity by 2032, according to Jayant Acharya, the chief executive officer and joint managing director of JSW Steel.

items.title: Online compliance to speed up environmental clearance for industrial expansion
items.summary: The ministry of environment, forest and climate change has made the process of obtaining certified compliance reports for industrial expansion proposals digital through the existing PARIVESH portal, aiming to ease procedures for businesses seeking environmental clearance.

items.title: Swatch stores close for second day after crowds
items.summary: The launch of a new £335 pocket watch has drawn criticism after queues of shoppers built up again.

items.title: Vodafone Idea gets promoter support but funding challenge persists
items.summary: Vodafone Idea posted early signs of recovery with stabilising subscribers and higher Arpu, backed by a ₹4,730-crore promoter infusion. However, analysts say large spectrum dues, funding gaps and network expansion needs remain major challenges.

items.title: G7 finance ministers to meet amid warning of economic consequences of prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure
items.summary: G7 finance ministers are due to meet in Paris on Monday and Tuesday as long-term borrowing costs surge.

items.title: West Bengal rice industry seeks infra push, faster clearances from new BJP govt
items.summary: West Bengal's rice sector urges the new state government for better infrastructure and quicker approvals. Industry leaders highlight the need for improved rural roads, electricity, and a single-window clearance system. They also advocate for a dedicated Rice Industry Development Board. Enhanced rail and port connectivity is crucial for boosting exports of premium rice varieties like Gobindo Bhog and Tulai Panji.

items.title: Realty group Sattva deepens premium hotel push amid travel boom
items.summary: Sattva’s expansion comes amid a broader shift among Indian real estate developers towards hospitality assets as room rates and occupancies remain high after the pandemic-led travel rebound.

items.title: India, Chile expedite CEPA negotiations and critical minerals cooperation
items.summary: Chile's Foreign Minister Francisco Pérez Mackenna's visit to India boosted bilateral ties, focusing on CEPA negotiations, critical minerals, and investment. Discussions with Indian ministers and a business delegation explored expanding economic cooperation and strategic convergence. The visit also highlighted a shared commitment to innovation and digital collaboration.

items.title: Boos, shouts and a Google billionaire: What happened when Eric Schmidt tried to talk AI at a 2026 graduation
items.summary: Students at the University of Arizona booed former Google chief executive Eric Schmidt during his commencement address after he spoke about artificial intelligence and job automation.

items.title: India's trade deficit pressures could persist through 2026 as electronics imports surge, export outlook stays fragile
items.summary: Indias trade deficit faces pressure in coming months. Elevated crude prices and supply disruptions impact exports. A potential global demand slowdown adds to concerns. Rupee depreciation may offer some support. A recent hike in bullion import duty could provide near-term relief to the deficit. Electronics deficit reached an all-time high.

items.title: FTAs, lower import duties, better business environment to boost net FDI flows: ADB chief economist
items.summary: Foreign investment in India needs a boost. ADB Chief Economist Albert Park suggests free trade agreements and lower import tariffs. Strengthening manufacturing infrastructure and urban planning will attract more capital. Higher oil prices are expected to persist, impacting India's GDP growth and inflation. Reforms are ongoing, and continued momentum is advised.

items.title: India taps Dutch tech, elevates ties to strategic partnership
items.summary: India and the Netherlands have forged a strategic partnership, signing 17 agreements to enhance collaboration. Discussions focused on defense, critical minerals, and global trade disruptions. Both nations emphasized freedom of navigation and a peaceful Indo-Pacific. Cooperation will expand in green hydrogen, semiconductors, and defense manufacturing. Bilateral trade and investment are set to grow significantly.

items.title: North America, NE Asia, Latin America account for over 35 pc of India's exports in FY26
items.summary: India's exports reached USD 441.78 billion in 2025-26. North America, North-East Asia, and Latin America together accounted for over 35 percent. Exports grew significantly in Asia, Africa, and Latin America. North-East Asia saw a surge of 21.6 percent. Indian exporters penetrated 1,821 new principal commodity products. Advanced engineering and industrial sectors contributed the most value.

items.title: Bain Capital closes largest Asia fund after raising $10.5bn
items.summary: Buyout firm raised $2.1bn more from external investors than had been targeted

items.title: How Britain can tackle its mountain of unpaid energy bills
items.summary: A long-term plan such as a social tariff could stop vulnerable households from running up debt

items.title: Record high Japanese yields trigger bets on repatriation
items.summary: Fund managers say country’s investors will sell out of US Treasuries to invest in JGBs

items.title: Chinese data centre spin-off plans dual IPO in Singapore and US
items.summary: DayOne aims to raise $5bn using new rules intended to boost Asian listings in city-state

items.title: India's demand-side push could build USD 37.8 billion forex shield as high oil prices threaten fiscal stability: Brickwork Ratings
items.summary: With crude prices likely to stay above USD 100 per barrel for the rest of 2026 and the rupee under pressure near INR 95/USD, Brickwork Ratings says Prime Minister Modi's seven behavioural appeals could provide India with a USD 37.8 billion forex buffer this fiscal year. The report argues that voluntary demand reduction across fuel, gold and fertilizers may offer the government fiscal breathing room at a time when its ability to absorb oil price pass-through is thinning.

items.title: This tech job grew 729% in a year amid AI-related layoffs. Salaries now touch ₹1.7 crore
items.summary: Forward-deployed engineer job postings surged 729% between April 2025 and April 2026, with salaries topping $200,000. Anthropic, OpenAI, Palantir and McKinsey are all hiring. Here is what the role involves and why demand is exploding.

items.title: MoSPI proposes framework to measure India's knowledge economy contribution
items.summary: The Centre has proposed a framework to measure India’s knowledge economy, covering AI, R&D, software, innovation and digital assets, amid growing focus on intangible economic contributions.

items.title: Silver imports restricted; licence now must for select categories
items.summary: The Centre has restricted imports of certain silver categories, including high-purity silver bars, making licences mandatory to curb rising imports and protect foreign exchange reserves.

items.title: Robert paid £726 to skip the driving test waiting list. New laws mean others won't be able to
items.summary: The government is cracking down on third parties who use bots to buy tests and resell them at inflated prices.

items.title: Venezuela Circulates Draft of Oil Law Regulations for Companies
items.summary: The Venezuelan government is circulating a draft of regulations as part of its newly enacted oil law, highly anticipated by companies planning to invest in the country’s increased oil production.
